presentation_options.text: What is different in meaning between “com” and “com”? What does the extra part do?
Starting from “com” (meaning ‘com’), how do you say it when it is a different person, number, or tense?
If we change the subject from “com” to “com”, does “com” change? How?
Inside “com”, is “com” a piece you can take off and still have a word, or does it only ever appear stuck on?
Is there a difference between “com” and “com”? Do they mean different things, or are they the same word said two ways?
Can you say “com”? Does it sound right / natural to you?
Which of these sounds most natural: ‘com’? Are any of them wrong?
